commit.message:
[Agent] SwiftData Task 6: Fix fetchLimit and wire ModelContainer to all hosted views (#2570)

* feat: add SwiftDataContinuesDataDriver and wire ModelContainer to all hosting controllers

- Add SwiftDataContinuesDataDriver that sets fetchLimit on FetchDescriptor
  before fetching (avoids materializing large result sets in memory)
- Add makeHostingController helper on PVRootViewController that injects
  the shared ModelContainer into every hosted SwiftUI view hierarchy
- Replace all bare UIHostingController(rootView:) calls in
  PVRootViewController and its extensions with makeHostingController()
  so @Query works in continues management sheets and all other flows

Part of #2568

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review issues in SwiftData Task 6

- Use `Any?` instead of `(any AnyObject)?` for sharedModelContainer to
  use conventional Swift type-erased storage (avoids existential syntax
  for a simple type-erasure pattern)
- Add `onTermination` handler to SwiftDataContinuesDataDriver's
  AsyncStream so the background fetch task is cancelled when the
  consumer stops listening (mirrors RealmContinuesDataDriver behaviour)
- Build FetchDescriptor with predicate at init time, then set
  fetchLimit — makes clear that the limit applies to filtered results
- Replace `self?.makeHostingController ?? UIHostingController(…)` with
  explicit guard-let in the Free ROMs closure so the model container is
  always injected and the fallback path is eliminated

Part of #2568

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix FOREIGN KEY crash on anonymous sessions and suppress builtin-tool startup noise (#9)

* Initial plan

* fix: FOREIGN KEY crash on anonymous sessions and suppress Twilio startup warning

- sessions.py: save_context() now does INSERT OR IGNORE to auto-create
  a parent sessions row (named 'anon-<prefix>') before inserting context
  entries, preventing the FOREIGN KEY constraint failure that occurs when
  kernel.run() is called with an anonymous UUID session (one not
  pre-registered via get_or_create).

- kernel/__init__.py: pass None as drivers when loading builtin example
  tools so their setup() is not invoked at startup. Builtin tools are
  reference implementations; setup() should only run for user-installed,
  fully-configured tools. This eliminates the noisy Twilio missing-env-
  vars WARNING on every startup.

- tests/test_sessions.py: add test_save_context_anonymous_session to
  specifically cover the FK-constraint fix path.

Co-authored-by: aniolowie <182877490+aniolowie@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tools): add hard timeout safety net for shell processes

Every shell process now has a maximum lifetime ceiling (default 10 min,
configurable via :shell_max_lifetime app env). When exceeded, the process
is terminated automatically and the agent receives a clear error. The soft
checkpoint-based timeout remains the primary mechanism; the hard timeout
is a safety net against runaway commands the agent never stops.

- Schedule hard_timeout timer on process start
- Cancel timer on normal exit or manual termination
- Return hard_timeout tuple to all waiters on expiry
- Log a warning when hard timeout fires
- Format hard timeout as an actionable error in shell tool

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(CVE-2020-25084): add xHCI usb_packet_copy assertion PoC

- Add CVE-2020-25084/ directory with full exploit and README
- Root cause: xhci_setup_packet() ignores return value of xhci_xfer_create_sgl()
  and usb_packet_map(); when DATA TRB has direction mismatch, SGL is destroyed
  (nsg=0), usb_packet_map returns 0 but p->iov.size=0, then usb_packet_copy
  asserts (0+18<=0) -> SIGABRT
- Key discovery: unmapped PA (0xDEAD…) does NOT trigger because QEMU's
  address_space_map() uses bounce buffers for non-RAM regions (non-NULL return)
- Real trigger: DATA TRB with OUT direction (TRB_TR_DIR=0) for an IN transfer
  causes xhci_xfer_create_sgl direction-mismatch -> xhci_die -> empty SGL
- Update SKILL (reproduce-qemu-cve.md) with xHCI MMIO pattern, bounce buffer
  quirk, NEC xHCI SS_FIRST port layout, and HCSPARAMS1 field encoding
- Rename SKILL file from reproduce.md to reproduce-qemu-cve.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Create rpg-ywes subtasks: unified constraint system with physics integration

10 subtasks for Custom Skeletal Mesh Format with Pose Constraints:

1. rpg-qslo: Unified constraint type system (20 Blender-compatible types)
2. rpg-1blk: Space conversion and constraint evaluation core
3. rpg-s9ob: IK solvers (CCD, FABRIK, Spline IK)
4. rpg-augx: Transform copy and tracking constraints
5. rpg-9sjh: Limit constraints (rotation, location, scale)
6. rpg-ngt5: Transform mapping, action, child-of, pivot
7. rpg-pkgw: Surface/volume constraints (floor, clamp, shrinkwrap)
8. rpg-j8n0: Physics engine integration (ragdoll, bidirectional)
9. rpg-b17x: .fskel format and loader
10. rpg-v00q: Visual test battery (8 tests)

Key design decision: constraints are a UNIFIED system shared by both
the animation pose solver and physics rigid-body engine. Same
constraint_def_t drives both animation bones and physics bodies.

commit.message:
Add bind-pose computation and visual humanoid test

- Add gltf_scene_compute_bind_pose() to gltf_loader API
  Walks cgltf joint hierarchy to compute joint_world × IBM matrices
  Uses cgltf_node_transform_world() (column-major, no transpose needed)

- New file: src/renderer/gltf/gltf_bind_pose.c (1 public function)

- Add 3 unit tests for bind-pose computation (17/17 pass):
  compute_bind_pose, compute_bind_pose_null, compute_bind_pose_small_buf

- New visual test: tests/visual/p004_visual_humanoid.c
  Loads humanoid.glb, creates 12 skeletal meshes with skinning shader
  Custom GLSL 430 shader matching skeletal_mesh attribute layout (6/7)
  Raw SSBO bone palette with bind-pose matrices
  Orbit camera, 90 frames at 30fps, video capture + PPM snapshots
